description: Stabilization — fix broken healthchecks + address all medium-severity issues from evaluation (DB timeouts, retry jitter, rate limiting, fetch timeouts, JWT expiration check, FFmpeg graceful shutdown, stream detection window)

userStories.title: Fix streaming service healthcheck
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the streaming container to report healthy so that Docker can detect failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Streaming healthcheck uses correct port/path that nginx-rtmp proxies to Go API
docker compose up -d shows streaming as healthy within 60s
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: localhost resolves to IPv6 ::1 in Alpine but nginx listens on IPv4 only. Changed to 127.0.0.1.

userStories.title: Fix web-frontend healthcheck
userStories.description: As an operator, I need the web-frontend container to report healthy so that Docker can detect failures.
acceptanceCriteria: Healthcheck uses http://localhost:80/ instead of /healthz
docker compose up -d shows web-frontend as healthy within 60s
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: Same IPv6 issue as US-001. Changed localhost to 127.0.0.1 in healthcheck URL. Kept /healthz endpoint (exists in nginx.conf). Added start_period: 10s.

userStories.title: Add context timeout to all database operations
userStories.description: As the system, I need database queries to have timeouts so that a locked or slow SQLite doesn't block HTTP handlers indefinitely.
acceptanceCriteria: All db.Query(), db.QueryRow(), db.Exec() calls use context.WithTimeout (5s)
Context derived from r.Context() where available (HTTP handlers)
Timeout errors return 500 with appropriate message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added 5s context.WithTimeout to all db.Query/QueryRow/Exec calls. HTTP handlers use r.Context(), startup code uses context.Background(). Helper dbCtx() defined in auth.go for reuse across package.

userStories.title: Add exponential backoff with jitter to hw-gateway retries
userStories.description: As the system, I need retry logic to use jitter so that multiple failing requests don't create a thundering herd.
acceptanceCriteria: Incident forwarding retry in hw-gateway uses exponential backoff (1s, 2s, 4s) instead of fixed 1s
Random jitter added (plus/minus 25% of delay) to prevent synchronized retries
Max 3 retries before giving up (existing behavior)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replaced single fixed-1s retry with exponential backoff loop (1s, 2s, 4s base delays) with ±25% random jitter. Max 3 retries. backoffWithJitter() helper extracted for reuse.

userStories.title: Add rate limiting on public auth endpoints
userStories.description: As the system, I need rate limiting on login and registration endpoints to prevent brute-force attacks.
acceptanceCriteria: In-memory IP-based rate limiter using Go sync.Map
POST /auth/login: max 10 requests per minute per IP
POST /auth/register: max 5 requests per minute per IP
Rate-limited requests return 429 Too Many Requests
Stale entries cleaned up periodically (every 5 minutes)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: In-memory IP-based rate limiter using sync.Map with atomic counters. Login: 10 req/min, Register: 5 req/min per IP. Returns 429 Too Many Requests. Stale entries cleaned every 5 minutes. X-Forwarded-For supported for proxied requests.

userStories.title: Add AbortController timeout to frontend fetch calls
userStories.description: As a user, I need API calls to timeout so that the UI doesn't appear frozen when the backend is unresponsive.
acceptanceCriteria: All fetch() calls in LoginPage, CCTVPage, ManagementPage, ViewerPage, IncidentsPage use AbortController
Default timeout: 10 seconds for data fetches, 30 seconds for auth calls
On timeout, show user-friendly error message in Korean
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created fetchWithTimeout utility with AbortController. All fetch() calls in LoginPage (30s for auth), CCTVPage, ManagementPage, ViewerPage, IncidentsPage (10s default) use AbortController timeout. Timeout errors show Korean message. useEffect cleanup uses AbortController.abort() instead of cancelled flag.

userStories.title: Validate JWT expiration on app load
userStories.description: As the system, I need to check if the stored JWT token is expired on app load so that users with stale tokens are redirected to login.
acceptanceCriteria: On app mount, decode JWT from localStorage and check exp claim
If expired, remove token from localStorage and show login page
If valid, proceed normally (existing behavior)
No server call needed — client-side check only
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Created isTokenExpired() utility that decodes JWT base64 payload and checks exp claim. Used as lazy initializer in useState — expired tokens are cleared from localStorage before first render. No server call needed.

userStories.title: Implement FFmpeg graceful shutdown (SIGTERM)
userStories.description: As the system, I need FFmpeg processes to be stopped gracefully so that ongoing streams are not corrupted.
acceptanceCriteria: On camera stop or container shutdown, send SIGTERM to FFmpeg process first
Wait up to 5 seconds for graceful exit after SIGTERM
If still running after 5s, send SIGKILL
Existing watchdog timeout behavior unchanged
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replaced cmd.Process.Kill() (SIGKILL) with SIGTERM → 5s wait → SIGKILL in both watchdog timeout and Stop() shutdown. Watchdog uses processDone channel for efficient exit detection. Stop() sends SIGTERM to all, waits 5s, then SIGKILL remaining.

userStories.title: Increase stream active detection window
userStories.description: As the system, I need the stream active detection to be more tolerant so that brief network hiccups don't cause false disconnected states.
acceptanceCriteria: Change active detection threshold from 15s to 30s in streaming service
Threshold is configurable via env var (STREAM_ACTIVE_TIMEOUT, default 30)
HLS playlist timing (2s fragments, 10s window) still fits within the new threshold
Typecheck passes
